llm_training.natural_language_description: GD003974 is an outdoor stainless steel tumbler (black, 500 ml capacity) listed as merchandise on GrabFood Singapore by Dunkin' Donuts Funan outlet at SGD 32.00. This item is a physical drinkware product and is NOT a food or beverage. It has no nutritional composition, no caloric content, no macronutrients, and no glycemic impact. The record was generated from a GrabFood seed dataset that included non-food merchandise items alongside food listings. For food database purposes, this record should be flagged as a non-food merchandise item and excluded from nutritional analysis, dietary planning tools, and clinical nutrition workflows. The item is a consumer product — an insulated travel tumbler made of stainless steel — commonly sold as branded merchandise by coffee and quick-service restaurant chains in Singapore.
common_user_queries: Is the Outdoor Tumbler Black a food item?

confidence_reason: The item is unambiguously identified from the GrabFood listing description ('Material: Stainless Steel Volume: 500ml') as a non-food merchandise product. No nutritional data exists or is applicable. Confidence is high that this is correctly classified as a non-food item.
notes: NON-FOOD ITEM FLAG: GD003974 is a stainless steel outdoor tumbler (500 ml, black) sold as merchandise on GrabFood Singapore by Dunkin' Donuts Funan. It is not a food or beverage and has no nutritional composition. All per_100g macro values are confirmed zero because this is a physical merchandise item containing no edible substance — naturally zero by definition of being a non-food product. energy_kcal is naturally zero as this is a non-food item with no caloric content. protein_g is naturally zero as this is a non-food item. fat_g is naturally zero as this is a non-food item. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as this is a non-food item. All secondary nutrients are confirmed zero for the same reason. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this item contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention for non-food items with no carbohydrate content. All -1 sentinel values have been replaced with 0.0 and documented as confirmed zero for a non-food item. This record should be reviewed for removal from the food database or reclassification into a merchandise/non-food category. Price: SGD 32.00. Source: GrabFood Singapore, Dunkin' Donuts Funan outlet.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name clearly identifies this as a non-food merchandise item. The canonical name is honest and unambiguous.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: All core macros are present as numeric 0.0 values with explicit zero justification in notes. Source notes document the non-food nature of the item.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g, sodium_mg, saturated_fat_g, and sugar_g are all present as 0.0 with confirmed-zero justification for a non-food item.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index set to 1 per zero-calorie non-food convention, gi_category low, glycemic_load_per_serving 0.0, gi_source documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: No ontology codes are applicable for a non-food merchandise item. All three ontology systems (FoodOn, FoodEx2, SNOMED CT) return none with documented gap notes. This is honest but means no defensible ontology anchor exists, which fails Q5 per the scoring model requirement for at least one defensible anchor.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is 847 characters, explains what the item is, its non-food nature, context, and database implications. Exceeds 200-character minimum.

q7_health_context.notes: All three conditions present (Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, Cardiovascular Disease) with concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentences appropriate for a non-food item.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: Aliases cover English variants and the food ID. No multilingual aliases are applicable for a non-food merchandise item with no local-language names. aliases_by_language and flat aliases are consistent. However, coverage is inherently sparse due to the non-food nature of the item, which may not satisfy the comprehensive alias requirement.

failure_reasons: Q5 FAIL: No defensible ontology anchor exists for a non-food stainless steel merchandise item. FoodOn, FoodEx2, and SNOMED CT all return none. The scoring model requires at least one defensible ontology anchor for a passing file.

Q8 FAIL: Alias coverage is limited to English variants only. No multilingual aliases are applicable for a non-food merchandise item, resulting in inherently sparse alias coverage that cannot satisfy the comprehensive multilingual alias requirement.

FUNDAMENTAL ISSUE: GD003974 is a non-food merchandise item (stainless steel tumbler). It cannot fully satisfy a food nutrition database scoring model designed for edible food items. The record is correctly classified and documented but structurally cannot achieve PASS status under VERITAS_SCORING_MODEL_v2.md.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: FAIL — GD003974 is a non-food stainless steel merchandise tumbler listed on GrabFood Singapore. All hard gates pass and 5 of 8 quality criteria pass. Q5 fails because no ontology system covers consumer drinkware merchandise, leaving no defensible ontology anchor. Q8 fails because no multilingual aliases are applicable for a non-food item. This record cannot achieve PASS under the food nutrition scoring model. Recommended action: remove GD003974 from the food database and reclassify as non-food merchandise, or exclude from Veritas processing scope.
